title: cordia tiliifolia
intro: Cordia tiliifolia, also known as the wild sage or bergamot bush, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as digestive issues and skin conditions. Cordia tiliifolia is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties.
